# notes

**learning**

roadmap:

* my own experiences  
* use good resources (there are many, search for best ones)  
* sources:  
* ppl  
* can bypass using recs from good ppl  
* places where there is just good quality content (also do search for these)  
* curius 👀  
* cool visualizations and instructive interactive displays  
* like wtf?? [https://ciechanow.ski/moon/fwe](https://ciechanow.ski/moon/fwe)  
* also very good for math (commutative diagrams; limit points; smash product) *Smash product \- Wikipedia* — [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smash\_product\#/media/File:Visualization\_of\_the\_smash\_product\_of\_two\_circles.gif](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smash_product#/media/File:Visualization_of_the_smash_product_of_two_circles.gif)  
* exceptional blogs  
* resource hubs  
* mit opencourse ware usually  
* ppl have reviews on the classes available somtimes: *MIT Challenge \- Scott H Young* — [https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/](https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/)  
* *HyperPhysics Concepts* — [http://www.hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/index.html](http://www.hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/index.html)  
* online stanford / other university lectures  
* *How to make the most of CS Primer* — [https://csprimer.com/how/](https://csprimer.com/how/)  
* *GitHub \- metacademy/metacademy-content: content repository for* [metacademy.org](https://metacademy.org) *· GitHub* — [https://github.com/metacademy/metacademy-content](https://github.com/metacademy/metacademy-content)  
* google images / image generation?  
* use lm to search  
* make searching prompts for language models  
* not only do you need good resources, you want resources that fit you well  
* esp without mentor, this is hard but can use experiences and reflections to see  
* very much the thing is read until can exercise / apply / implement and then go do that (although the actual reading part here is very important)  
* \*\*LEARN-FOR-AN-APPLICATION, THEN IMMEDIATELY APPLY.\*\* MVC → GRIFFITHS → EDDY CURRENTS WAS A MISSED APPLICATION WINDOW HARRISON EXPLICITLY REGRETS. THE LEARNING STICKS WHEN THE APPLICATION IS QUEUED UP. (THIS THIS THIS THIS)  
* ai is good for knowledge & searching for sources, needs to be used right  
* for using ai for edu:  
* basically we are targetting 24/7 personal tutor  
* usually explanations are pretty good  
* a couple of issues:  
* visualizations sometimes bad ←→ no whiteboard for teacher to draw stuff on / for you to draw with that is actually integrated so they can give you feedback  
* trained to be chatbot so the \[turn taking dynamics\] and \[intention/guidance\] are much worse than a teacher  
* sometimes they may not completely understand what you want to learn because you dont know how to describe it  
* persistence of info and instantiation of personalized knowledge (what do you alr know and after each session / other stuff how to keep it updated)  
* absolute correctness—optimal for teaching  
* when chatting with llms a good pattern is ask q's then explain back, check if its right  
* voice is esp good for this, can mimic a 1-1 convo with a teacher  
* rn what ive done is all pretty poor and involves just asking q's and repeating back in my own words → check if its right  
* not at all like an actual teacher where they are holding back info, structuring responses for my learning, joking around, controling speed deliberately, noticing how im feeling and changing explanations, etc  
* a lot of effort on my part to actually learn  
* learning thing for cs: theoretically what i should do for cs edu and vibe coding is to have the coding agent act as a natural language compiler and have it implement exactly what I tell it to implement. I need to provide all of the specific details; that way I bypass learning syntax, but still need to have the specifics for correct implementation. Otherwise, my code will not work. \#reflection \#arkansas \#learning   
* \*\*THE 3-STEP LEARNING ARCHITECTURE.\*\* "(1) COME UP WITH THEORIES ABOUT HOW THINGS WORK BY READING, (2) TEST THOSE HYPOTHESES, (3) MAINTAIN CONNECTION BETWEEN THEORY AND IMPLEMENTATION — SYNTAX ISN'T IMPORTANT." THIS IS THE LOOP FOR EVERY PROJECT: EE (MOSFETS → CIRCUIT → DEBUG), MATH (MUNKRES → PICTURES → PROOFS), ANESTHETICS (PAPERS → CNNS → VALIDATE). READING IS STEP 1, NOT AN AFTERTHOUGHT.  
* for reading and spitting things out, another thing that is decent is to take a note in my own words and basically just write up a doc after i learn the thing (like  bitcoin a network of nodes (software on anything—servers, computers, even a pi) that each have a ledger (the entire history of all transactions which is now around 600gb) when people want to make transactions, they take that info (bc the money is literally just info) and propogate it across the network until it reaches the mempools (a storage area in each node for verified (which means that there are no blatant errors like bad signature (e.g. if you wanted to take money from someone they need to use their private key to sign it and can be verified by a public key but if they didnt actually do it you cant fake it) or spending money you cant spend) but unconfirmed tx's which then miners then pick up and do work to prove. when making a transaction, you pay a fee which is usually around 1 satoshi per some amount of data (sat; 10^-8 BTC) and you determine that when making transaction the miners take a buncha transactions (they can choose which ones and just prioritize the oens with the largest fees, capped at 4mb of total tx data) and add on a subsidy tx that just gives them some amount of btc (as of apr 2026 its 3.125 which is around 225k?) and computes SHA256 hashes of the block header (a merkle (hash each transaction, then pair them up in a binary tree and hash all the way down, and then the final hash is the merkle) of the tx, nonce, etc) and when they find something numerically under some mining target (e.g. 10^20; the output is from 0 to 2^256) they can broadcast the block and the nodes, seeing that the broadcaster's chain is longest (assumingly n+1) and can verify everything (bc computing hash is easy), they accept it (add it into their chain \+ broadcast it) this prevens people from sending money for something and they reverting that send transaction because to independently add a block to the server network (and there are like hella of them) you would need to do the compute that so many machines are doing 24/7 before they finish it (by which the chain will grow too long for your one extra fradulent block to matter) which would be highly impossible qs: how did this thing get started? when there wasnt hella compute on it; was it some big investment → buy hella gpu time? actually it does seem plausible, esp with a higher mining target and a lower value to the btc how does the price fluctuate? what is up with UTXO and why do we need it where do ZKP come in what happens when two things are the same length and come in at the same time (e.g. X and Y propoage from different locations; then maybe the population of nodes is split? which is prob \#learning \#read  or  full llm each word gets embedded (sometimes pretrained embeddings models, more modern models have them trained end to end like a matrix E (d x V) where V is vocabulary size) to vectors (d dimensions) attention each vector runs through trained matrices M\_K (d\_k x d), M\_Q (d\_k x d), M\_V (d\_v x d) attention is like Query (a token that needs context infused into it) "is attended to by" Key (the identifier) and gets the Value (info; the secret sauce and tea) they are put in a grid against itself, one side is the Query, one side is the Key, then dot products are taken and then normalized and then softmaxed per query then Value is multiplied to the softmaxed probability per query (which represents "how likely another token is important for this token") in the grid, before softmax, add the causal mask in which you add \-inf to any QK pair that looks into the future (e.g. a Q that is index i and a K that is index i \+ 1); i think index i and index i is ok this is only for decoder even though earlier tokens wont be able to tend to later tokens, that doesnt mean they wont interact (later tokens tend to earlier ones) formalized: softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d\_k)) V divide by sqrt(d\_k) because the dot products are sums of stuff with var 1 → var d\_k, then divide by sqrt(d\_k) brings var back to 1 which is good for softmax the output is a list of n vectors in d\_v (this could end here by setting d\_v \= d, but\!) you can also have multihead attention in which you get h of these heads with their own matrices then get all hd\_v guys, concatenate them together, project them using matrix from (n, h d\_v) to (n, d) mlp W2 sigma(W1 x) usually W1 projects x into a higher space (usually 4 \* d) and then W2 projects it back to d layernorm within each n tokens: get mu and sigma also shift by beta and scale by gamma (learned scalars) (just throw a basic linear transformation in there for fun) epsilon in the denom in the sqrt bc why not formalized: gamma \* (x \- mu) / sqrt(sigma^2 \+ e) \+ beta run initial embedding through L y1 \= x \+ attention(layernorm(x)); y2 \= y1 \+ mlp(layernorm(y1)) at the end we get n hidden states (out: (n, d)) which we convert into token probabilities using a unembedding (usually the transpose of the embedding matrix) then softmax the last index for probabilities then can sample in many ways then get token, append to list, go again. encoder takes in the entire thing and tends to all things with no causal mask; spits out contextualized tended embeddings (e.g. sentence 2 vec or doc 2 vec or bert) cant generate text or anything, but paired with decoder can translate etc and generate text but also just decoder is good for just generating text other things: position is actually not encoded and needs to be added separately (e.g. a simple one is just a rando sine wave with different frequencies depending on index??) with an input of n tokens, we get n hidden states after the entire transformer is done thus can get n training samples (1, 1 → 2, 1, 2 → 3, 1...n-1 → n) kv cache because of the causal mask, when you run a forward pass and then run another one with an extra token on the end, K and V for the previous guys dont change at all thus when running attention, compute K, Q, V for the new token, run attention on that new token using K, Q, V and the previous K and V lists for previous tokens, and get the hidden state value for the newest token, append it to the previous hidden state vectors (add new K and new V to the K and V lists) then can run mlp as usual makes time complexity much better big memory use this creates two phases of inference prefill (send the entire prompt in in one go and build the cache) decode (generate more text in response to a prompt; e.g. respond to a question) this doesnt work on encoders like bert bc all of the hidden states change all the time (image) from ( https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.06266 ) https://claude.ai/chat/dbfec710-9f94-49c1-9763-9d33f5e497b5 \#llms \#publishonwebsite \#exploration \#explanation  )  
* effort\!\!\! big effort, learning is hard, grind  
* one of the reasons that mathacademy is so good is that it just makes you do the computations  
* this is clearly corroborated by colemak ←→ qwerty; it just takes some time and the willpower to grind through slow typing exercises while not looking at keys  
* also math kinda  
* use [https://www.moonflowers.xyz/writing/how\_confidence\_changed\_my\_life](https://www.moonflowers.xyz/writing/how_confidence_changed_my_life) etc; do the thing (esp having the system tell you what to do makes it much easier and therefore less uncomfortable)  
* apple-notes/notes/learning-max-log.md:114-122. nov 1: "when learning it is common to be uncomfortable — not getting it, being slow, output is bad. THIS IS VERY COMMON IDK WHY IM SO SAD WHEN MY STUFF AT THE BEGINNING IS SUPER SLOW. just grind through it. school is an amazing place to practice these skills."  
* **when reading, its much more efficient to have info be organized**  
* In classes, this is already the case; in other writing pieces and or sources it usually is the case (like textbooks)  
* but sometimes it is not necessarily the case and/or you want to mix lots of sources  
* then a mentor / ai figure with more knowledge and experience would be highly helpful to org it and plan it out with you  
* e.g. hamstudy just memorized individual facts instead of doing things in batches (there were such good batches with principles and laws i could have learned)  
* e.g. for stats dont be like ok so proportion CI, proportion significance test, 2 proportion ...  
* instead, 2 different objectives:  
* around what is p hat? how can we engineer a range so that we can be more safe with our claims?  
* hypothesis testing (very well motivated)  
* known quantities: estimated sigma p hat, p hat, p, etc → how to devise prob that we see something  
* we want such an estimate to satisfy x, y, z  
* this is kinda the higher math way of doing it (definitions, make propositions, prove everything, slowly build real intuition) and i kinda like it  
* this would have been much better for me and probably for a lot of people  
* **target deep intuitive and instinctual understanding, makes everything else later so much easier; also makes the learning so much useful in the future (wont forget it, create connections across disciplines, etc)**  
* e.g. when learning stuff really have rock solid understanding of the base mats, bias towards that (even though may be a bit boring)  
* i hypothesize that just like the michael nielsen anki thing, doing those rote computations and examples etc in a disciplined way was what made me so good at math  
* constantly having to retrieve definitions of things and notation → friction  
* can make system to instantly pull up defs or hints  
* literally anki thing while reading: [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Incremental\_reading](https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Incremental_reading)  
* deep understanding: [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Do\_not\_memorize\_before\_you\_understand](https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Do_not_memorize_before_you_understand)  
* generalization is math academy "prerequisites", learn previous knowledge well before moving on (one of the reasons group edu is really hard)  
* **getting a mentor is highly important and will crazily elevate learnings**  
* cold email ppl, ask around circiles (lol referral.bike but referral.bike is super cold)  
*  exceptional tutor finding platform Building onto the idea of something like, um... The learning optimization, like, learning sweet. Um, it would be important, probably. to have something that allows people to find, like, really, really good tutors. who have, um, I think it's twofold, right?  Or maybe three, two and a half maybe. Uh, who have a lot of good experience, and are, like, actually good at things. That'll be easier to validate.  And then the second thing is, like, they teach well, which, like, maybe it could be, like, Oh, they follow our pattern, and that's how we validate them. Or it could just be, like, reviews. Or a combination, right?  And then, somehow, like, actually make sure all of them are very, very good. And then, yeah, this might be, like, the ideal case. I don't know, something that already exists, and it's just hard to achieve the ideal case.  But if we do, it's better. But, uh, yeah, this is a good thought. \#learningsuite \#mentorship \#oldprojectidea    
*  its a bit ironic im looking for mentor figures that can really boost me through a subject; i have so many right in front of me that i dont appreciate teachers at school elie (who was willing to talk to me about anything and i actually so disrespect him) parents anyways it would be interesting to try to find very very good tutors for ap's instead of khan academy they exist wouldnt be that expensive given how little time ill need from them would actually accelerate my learning pretty nicely social interaction\! learn how to find a really good tutor; can do for other things \#learningsuite \#mentorship   
* without mentors, you need to just do random stuff instead of having guidance  
* revise what you need to do over time when you learn  
* **the social aspect certainly matters—mentor is only half the equation**  
* also classmates \+ teacher (which is a plus on school learning)  
* find people to talk about the learning with online or in person  
* just frequently look for ppl to talk to  
* from projects (90% can be rephrased to target learning):  
* people  
* \*\*Mismatched collaborators drain projects.\*\* Cost of fit-mismatch on a project \> logistical friction of working alone.  
* highly aligned collaborators / advisor type people (“advisory type” is very broad, could be a good friend that cares about you and is curious about your proj) are very good  
* a really good way to hit so many positive things (learn, grow, meet ppl, achieve / brand) is  
* frequently talks to others about it  
* good for community and social  
* good for getting more ideas on what to do  
* good for practicing explaining in your own words, practicing articulation  
* → a generalization of typical research where you check in with pi often and startup land where talk to advisors, investors, and customers; nueva project where you talk to friends and teacher abt it  
* this could just be like "i made a thing, what do you think" to a friend or mentor, another reason why doing stuff in mentors' spaces is good (←→ research mentorship)  
* “\*\*Ideal state: grind → chat w/ ppl (or reflect or something) → grind.\*\* Every flow state in the reflections (break-those-blocks, databox, mug warmer, eeg, pause, wikihub sprint) had fast feedback \+ external checkpoints. Pick projects with short feedback loops or engineer loops in.”  
* “Famous open-door point: Shannon's closed-office peers were productive short-term; Shannon worked with the door open and stayed relevant longer because he paid the "price of admission" to hear what problems mattered.”  
* \*\*Hacker / intellectual houses are top environments.\*\* Natural byproduct: good collaborators, fast feedback, social grounding in one place.  
* \*\*HUXLEY: INDEX ON KIND/PROACTIVE, NOT SIMILAR-GOALS.\*\* WHEN PICKING COLLABORATORS. FIT ON TEMPERAMENT \> FIT ON TOPIC.  
* If in team: • • RESYNC OFTEN (CLARIFY EXPECTATIONS, REPORT PROGRESS)  
* People can help hella  
* [https://www.moonflowers.xyz/writing/learning\_understanding\_connections](https://www.moonflowers.xyz/writing/learning_understanding_connections)  
* Also mentors in the field can guide you to cool problems  
* [https://www.benkuhn.net/11/](https://www.benkuhn.net/11/)  
* **debugging mistakes in understanding**  
* *Andy Matuschak \- Self-Teaching, Spaced Repetition, Why Books Don’t Work* — [https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/andy-matuschak](https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/andy-matuschak)  
* ← active reading. asking questions and answering them; constantly self testing for the info ←→ testing effect from mathacaedmy  
* meta cognition is usually hard when grappling with hard mats so outsource it to syllabus etc  
* textbooks have questions/exercises → can make llm to write questions for notes / textbooks, inject them ←→ make anki cards w/ quesiotns for things  
* **finding connections to previous content**  
* this is pretty clear, highly corroborated  
* *MIT Challenge \- Scott H Young* — [https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/](https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/)  
* in terms of note taking, connections are hella good  
* wikipedia / llm wiki style; im sure org-mode / jack has some good stuff for this  
* ideaflow is nice but like i too often want to refer to specific lines or sentences  
* creates connections in brain & understanding  
* will think of stuff, its good to remember it / note it down  
* need low friction for this kinda thing  
* \- knowledge compounds n² (ideaflow/2026-04-07\_also-another-reason-to-go-and-read…md). "everything is connected; the more you know it scales n^2." → quantitative justification for your "find connections to previous content" item. one liner you wrote, not in the excerpt, but it's the math behind why connection-finding matters.  
* **ensuring motivation and continuing motivation**  
* this is useful in a non optimized system / when im not motivated at all, but like intrinsically enjoying the thing is only useful so as to get me to participate in the well oiled system (and maybe do some of my own stuff / fix it if its not working well) but outside of that, if the system works well, there isnt ireally much for extra motivation to do  
* that said often times the full motivation is especially useful for some certain parts of the system that might be hard to participate in  
* makes it so that i constantly think about it xyz  
* so things in there that will give me more motivation? e.g. times in the learning where we take a break from hyper optimized stuff and slow down for some edutainment / inspiration to get my further hooked on it  
* ←→ talking people who also like the thing is really good for this; community great motivator (e.g. phd → department)  
* one of the things we can do is the savor in understanding and insight into the thing; dont just rush past it and be like "ok next thing"  
* keep an informal meta-log of the learning (esp important when no mentor; you are your own mentor)  
* what has been hard  
* what has been fun  
* how do to better, etc etc  
* what is the track i want to be on to do a certain task?  
* what more do i want to learn? what is the big question rn?  
* this should be done during the session but its hard be need to actively understand the stuff  
* sources  
* IF notes  
* ultralearning  
* i think the book itself isnt that good, gotta look at the examples  
* *Mastering Linear Algebra in 10 Days: Astounding Experiments in Ultra-Learning \- Cal Newport* — [https://calnewport.com/mastering-linear-algebra-in-10-days-astounding-experiments-in-ultra-learning/](https://calnewport.com/mastering-linear-algebra-in-10-days-astounding-experiments-in-ultra-learning/)  
* *MIT Challenge \- Scott H Young* — [https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/](https://www.scotthyoung.com/blog/myprojects/mit-challenge-2/)  
* math academy way principles (also the “what findings are solid”)  
* ![][image1]  
* anki type  
* feynman type  
* learning for classes from andrej: [https://cs.stanford.edu/people/karpathy/advice.html](https://cs.stanford.edu/people/karpathy/advice.html)  
* a really good \~2h video between andy matuschak  
* *Andy Matuschak \- Self-Teaching, Spaced Repetition, Why Books Don’t Work* — [https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/andy-matuschak](https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/andy-matuschak)  
* using a latex getter \+ in browser flashcard creator that can render latex to grind through a quantum mechanics textbook  
* [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/20\_rules\_of\_knowledge\_formulation](https://supermemo.guru/wiki/20_rules_of_knowledge_formulation)  
* decent but not insane: [https://medium.com/learn-love-code/learnings-from-learning-how-to-learn-19d149920dc4\#.jcklvssn4](https://medium.com/learn-love-code/learnings-from-learning-how-to-learn-19d149920dc4#.jcklvssn4)  
* a buncha them are in mathacademy way  
* illusions of competence is clear (although i ahvent written it up yet)  
* focused and diffused modes are good  
* lots of diffusion (meditation, sleeping, talking to people)  
* cool stuff lik e*Paperscape* — [https://paperscape.org](https://paperscape.org)  
* [https://www.whz.de/fileadmin/lehre/hochschuldidaktik/docs/dunloskiimprovingstudentlearning.pdf](https://www.whz.de/fileadmin/lehre/hochschuldidaktik/docs/dunloskiimprovingstudentlearning.pdf)  
* similar stuff to mathacademy etc; also funny that they are like "hey we are giving you **basic,** **easily implementable** principles" because they are so unimplemented  
* feynmans things  
* [https://medium.com/@gursahay24/surely-youre-joking-mr-feynman-280b8622acaf](https://medium.com/@gursahay24/surely-youre-joking-mr-feynman-280b8622acaf)  
* the 4 step method  
* have a thing  
* explain the thing in basic terms to someone younger than you  
* find gaps  
* fill those gaps  
* repeat  
* i think there is a discord community for this btw ↑  
* also:  
* writing down all questions and organizing info within big knowledge base, constantly rereading it  
* reinventing notation / rederiving from scratch  
* using different problem representations of a single thing and using different methods  
* no fake understanding ←→ explaining to a young child  
* write in my own lang  
* questions:  
* what is the tradeoff between too much spacing and too little spacing? esp for something like studying for a test where at the end i will have to know xyz knowledge, with the same time: 10h all at once? 1h / week? 5m a day? 1m every 3h (during the day only)?  
  * The spacing doesnt matter that much, but total time \+ time per session \+ deadline \+ start date determines spacing.  
    * Time per session should be increased based on the   
* how to take sources and quickly turn it into a graph or something so that the power memory algos can be run on it?  
* how do current learning optim things work? try out some ai for edu and mathacademy type things (physics graph sucks)  
* hmm what is up with the actual neuroscience? how much of findings is empirical and how much of it is consistent with dominant theory?  
* how to quantify learning to learn? learning can be semiquantified with tests etc, but how to really test? ig do the costly full test set eval of "do the thing i need to learn this to do" and see how good it is  
* connections across ideas are always good. however, if there isnt specific connection provided by text / material and there isnt one that specifically arises in my brain, should i go and think of one? maybe have llms make connections to known objects in knowledge base?  
* how to even make good knowledge base? rn the one i have is super super rough  
* how much does optimizations help past the really obvious ones? im in a class and just show up 2x a week, do no outside work, and can do the things  
  * so easy  
  * i wonder if the obvious ones will be a 95 5 or a 60 40; leaning towards 95 5\. then it wont be "i have super big thing that is hella good and non obvious", it will be "i have thing that is just super robust implementation of 10 big ideas in the public domain and works surprisingly well"  
* Even flashcards are annoying to make when learning is currently shaky because understanding isnt that deep \-\> if get llm to make its unfamiliar (also if a lot of content, llm to make is bad)  
  * Moral of the story: mixing llm \+ khan academy \+ flashcards is not it  
    * It should be llm takes info, makes curriculum \+ flashcards.  
    * After reading cretain curriculum, do flashcards  
* How do flashcards and course memorization work together with projects and just doing things? How to balance that (this is probably very unsolved ←-\> yolo just do it at some point when you feel comfortable)

quality sources prompt:

* that are high quality and are   
* 1\. concise and direct (absolutely no slop or half hearted)  
* 2\. centered in interest (wikipedia is pretty good for this, absolutely no blop posts from a company about that company's product)  
* 3\. has actually insightful/novel content and or a very wide summarizing breadth  
* 4\. prose is interesting and engaging  
* 5\. correct and accurate content (meaning updated and relevant)  
* 6\. highly corroborated either through people doing similar things to them or reviews are strong (this one is only for products etc)

\#learning

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
